last_substantive_baseline.title: Use ProviderNoRbac for read-only ARM planning
last_substantive_baseline.claim_boundary: This baseline proves the read-only planner completed current-state inventory, ProviderNoRbac validation, and What-If against the architecture at commit 19b8eddf4fb9038a41ba1fb0e81567dcbdfe2e92. It does not authorize Azure mutation, prove current pricing or quota, deploy report Storage, publish a report, or prove frontend live-data ingestion.

repository_observation.head_semantics: PR #40 merge commit observed before the Blob-endpoint browser-CORS repair branch was opened
repository_observation.claim_boundary: This is time-bounded external evidence. PR #42 was created after this observation. Resolve current head, pull requests, CI, and review state from live GitHub whenever this file is read.

authored_change.write_owner: ChatGPT conversation: Azure IaC MSP Lab — bounded Blob-endpoint browser-CORS architecture repair explicitly authorized by Anthony Edgar with 'Proceed'
authored_change.scope: Correct the public-report browser path from the Azure Storage static-website endpoint to the Blob service endpoint. Enable anonymous Blob access only on the dedicated sanitized-output account, declare the $web container with Blob-only public access, preserve managed-identity OAuth writes and exact-origin Blob-service CORS, update tests and documentation, and invalidate the prior three-create planner artifact for execution. No Azure authentication, deployment, Storage mutation, RBAC mutation, VM command, report publication, frontend source change, or workflow dispatch is permitted.
verification_criteria: publicReportUrl uses the Azure Blob service endpoint and the $web object path rather than primaryEndpoints.web
the dedicated sanitized-output account enables allowBlobPublicAccess while shared-key authorization remains disabled and OAuth remains the default write path
the explicit $web container resource uses publicAccess Blob and never Container
Blob-service CORS remains restricted to the reviewed HTTPS origin and GET, HEAD, and OPTIONS
versioning, seven-day delete retention, and Storage-scoped collector Blob Data Contributor remain declared
documentation states that CORS is not authentication and anonymous read applies only to sanitized public output
the successful three-create planner artifact is marked superseded for execution and a fresh four-create plan is required after merge
project validation, all unit tests, and Bicep lint/build pass for the exact pull-request head
the final diff remains limited to the nine declared paths
authored_change.failure_behavior: Keep PR #42 draft, inspect the exact failing test or CI job, patch only the declared paths, and obtain fresh exact-head CI. Do not weaken the sanitized-output boundary, broaden public access to Container, introduce wildcard CORS, enable shared-key writes, modify PR #41, grant Azure permissions, or perform Azure actions.
authored_change.rollback: Close PR #42 without merge or revert its repository-only commits. No Azure rollback or cleanup applies because this increment performs no Azure authentication or Azure mutation.

bounded_authority_grants.authorization_source: Explicit user instruction 'Proceed' after the read-only Azure planning boundary was stated
bounded_authority_grants.claim_boundary: This grant authorizes authentication and dispatch only for current-state inventory, ProviderNoRbac ARM validation, and What-If. It does not authorize deployment, role changes, report publication, endpoint configuration, guest commands, or any Azure resource mutation.

latest_observed_planner_attempt.supersession: The plan evaluated the static-website public URL and did not include an explicit $web container access resource. It is protected historical evidence and must not authorize deployment after PR #42 changes the architecture.

latest_promoted_evidence.freshness_boundary: This is the latest successfully completed report-publication plan but it evaluates the pre-repair architecture. A fresh plan against the exact merged PR #42 commit is required before proposing or authorizing deployment.

pr40_provider_no_rbac_repair.claim_boundary: Planner reliability and read-only Azure evidence only; not deployment permission, mutation authority, publication, or browser proof.

pr41_publication_execution_promotion.claim_boundary: Exact-head CI passed, but the workflow was pinned to the superseded three-create plan and static-website endpoint architecture. It must remain draft and be rebased or repinned after a fresh four-create plan.

pr42_blob_endpoint_repair.claim_boundary: Repository-only architecture repair. No Azure action or merge is authorized by this state record.

authority_defaults.rule: Defaults remain fail-closed. The validated read-only planning grant is a narrow exception for Azure reads and ProviderNoRbac planning only. Draft PRs do not create execution authority.

live_state_resolution.repository_head: Query the live GitHub default branch at read time.
live_state_resolution.pull_requests: Query live open pull requests, draft status, reviews, and mergeability at read time.
live_state_resolution.ci: Query workflow runs and jobs for the exact commit under evaluation.
live_state_resolution.azure: Use fresh authenticated Azure evidence; repository declarations and historical artifacts are insufficient.
